intro.speaker: narrator
intro.text: The guild hall is smaller than the stories said. Candle-smoke and old ledgers. A fire down to its last embers. A woman at the counter does not look up as you cross the threshold.

brenna-notices.speaker: brenna
brenna-notices.text: ...Another one. I'd hoped we were done for the season.

brenna-greet.speaker: brenna
brenna-greet.text: You came all the way to Ashenvale. That tells me something. What it tells me depends on what you say next.

choices.text: I've been to the cairn. I found Veyrin's book.
I found Veyrin. He has a message for you.
What will you do now, Brenna?
Tell me about the one you lost.
You watch me longer than you used to.
I heard there was work here.
I had nowhere else to go.

quest-hub.speaker: brenna
quest-hub.text: The board has postings. I have a few more that are not on the board. Depends on how deep you are willing to go.

choices.text: Anything about spiders in the depths?
I want to go deeper into Mossbarrow.
Tell me about the thing on the throne.

spider-nest-offer.speaker: brenna
spider-nest-offer.text: The lower floors of Mossbarrow have spinners — cave spiders, big ones. They breed fast. Three confirmed nests on the first depth. Clear them and I will mark it on the ledger.

spider-nest-accept.speaker: brenna
spider-nest-accept.text: Watch for the webs. They slow you, and the spiders do not.

wraith-hunt-offer.speaker: brenna
wraith-hunt-offer.text: Two wraiths. They drift through the cairn walls and ignore steel that is not true-edged. Something is anchoring them — not a haunt, something older. Kill them, and we will learn whether more follow.

wraith-hunt-accept.speaker: brenna
wraith-hunt-accept.text: They are cold, and quiet, and they do not warn you. Keep your guard up.

depths-explorer-offer.speaker: brenna
depths-explorer-offer.text: The guild needs a map of the third floor. No one has returned from it with one. If you reach the bottom and come back breathing, that alone is worth the contract.

depths-explorer-accept.speaker: brenna
depths-explorer-accept.text: Do not linger down there. See it, note the exits, and come back. Heroism is for people who do not owe me a report.

hollow-king-offer.speaker: brenna
hollow-king-offer.text: At the bottom of Mossbarrow, on a throne that should not exist, something sits. The old records call it the Hollow King. It does not sleep. It does not leave. And everything that dies down there seems to serve it.

hollow-king-offer-2.speaker: brenna
hollow-king-offer-2.text: It is not the source. It is the gatekeeper. Whatever empowered it lies further down — but clearing the way starts here. If you kill it, we learn what it was guarding. If you fail, I will add your name to the wall. Either way, the guild remembers.

hollow-king-accept.speaker: brenna
hollow-king-accept.text: Prepare well. That thing has killed better than you. It may kill you too. But you already knew that.

wolf-cull-turnin.speaker: brenna
wolf-cull-turnin.text: The woods are quieter now. Good. The farmers will sleep easier, though they will not say thank you. They never do.

wolf-cull-done.speaker: brenna
wolf-cull-done.text: Fifty gold, seventy-five experience. Honest pay for honest work. Check the board if you want more.

bone-collector-turnin.speaker: brenna
bone-collector-turnin.text: Two skeletons. The cairn is a little less restless. For now.

bone-collector-done.speaker: brenna
bone-collector-done.text: Eighty gold, one-twenty experience. The dead do not stay down in that place. Keep your blade ready.

spider-nest-turnin.speaker: brenna
spider-nest-turnin.text: Three nests cleared. The air down there might breathe a little easier. Might.

spider-nest-done.speaker: brenna
spider-nest-done.text: Sixty gold, a hundred experience. They will breed again. They always do. But you bought us time.

wraith-hunt-turnin.speaker: brenna
wraith-hunt-turnin.text: Two wraiths put down. That is good. That is better than I expected. The question is what was holding them here, and whether it still is.

wraith-hunt-done.speaker: brenna
wraith-hunt-done.text: Ninety gold, one-fifty experience. If you see more of them, tell me before the wall does.

depths-explorer-turnin.speaker: brenna
depths-explorer-turnin.text: You reached the third floor and came back. That puts you ahead of everyone else I have sent. Tell me what you saw — no, tell me later. I can see it in your face. It was bad.

depths-explorer-done.speaker: brenna
depths-explorer-done.text: Seventy gold, one-thirty experience. Rest. Then we talk about what sits at the bottom.

hollow-king-turnin.speaker: brenna
hollow-king-turnin.text: You... you did it. The thing on the throne. You ended it.

hollow-king-turnin-2.speaker: narrator
hollow-king-turnin-2.text: Brenna sets her ledger down. For the first time since you have known her, she has nothing in her hands. She looks at you the way one looks at a fire that has just gone out — not with relief, but with the understanding that the room is about to be very cold.

hollow-king-turnin-3.speaker: brenna
hollow-king-turnin-3.text: I sent four people before you. Their names are on that wall. I wrote the last one myself, with steady hands, because that is what a guildmaster does. I was ready to write yours.

hollow-king-turnin-4.speaker: brenna
hollow-king-turnin-4.text: Two hundred gold from the guild purse. Five hundred experience — more than I have ever signed off on. And your name goes on the board in a different color. The color for people who come back.

hollow-king-turnin-5.speaker: brenna
hollow-king-turnin-5.text: The depths are quieter, but not silent. Something deeper stirs. This was the first gate. There will be others. But you proved it can be done. That is enough. That is more than enough.

hollow-king-close.speaker: brenna
hollow-king-close.text: Go rest. You have earned it. But when you are ready — there is something beneath where the throne stood. A passage. Sealed, for now. We will need to open it eventually. And it will be you.

what-remains-turnin.speaker: narrator
what-remains-turnin.text: Brenna sets her quill down. She does not ask you to sit. She does not reach for the ledger. She waits — and in the waiting, you see something you have not seen in her before. Fear.

what-remains-2.speaker: brenna
what-remains-2.text: You found him. Alive. Tell me everything — no. Tell me the end first. What did he say?

what-remains-3.speaker: narrator
what-remains-3.text: You repeat the words as Veyrin gave them. The seal holds. For now.

what-remains-4.speaker: brenna
what-remains-4.text: For now. Yes. That sounds like him. Always precise, even when the precision is a kindness.

what-remains-5.speaker: brenna
what-remains-5.text: Two hundred gold. Five hundred experience. And something I do not give lightly — the truth. Veyrin and I were partners, once. Before the guild. Before Ashenvale. I sent you to find him because no one else could be trusted with what he knows.

what-remains-6.speaker: brenna
what-remains-6.text: He cannot leave. That means the seal is weaker than it should be. That means whatever is beneath it is waking up. Slowly — but it is waking.

what-remains-7.speaker: brenna
what-remains-7.text: There will be more work. Harder work. The kind that does not go on the board. When it comes, I will not ask — I will tell you. Because you are the one who came back. You are always the one who comes back.

what-remains-close.speaker: brenna
what-remains-close.text: Rest. Sharpen what needs sharpening. The next gate will not open itself.

return-shock.speaker: brenna
return-shock.text: You have it. Let me — no, do not hand it to me. Open it to the last entry. Point with your finger, not mine.

return-read.speaker: narrator
return-read.text: Brenna leans over the counter. She reads the entry, then reads it again. Her jaw tightens at a sigil drawn in the margin you had not noticed. When she straightens, her face is in control of itself — but you saw.

return-speak.speaker: brenna
return-speak.text: Veyrin did not write the last line. The hand is different. I know that hand. I thought I would never see it again.

return-offer.speaker: brenna
return-offer.text: You did the work. Eventually you'll go back to the cairn — not today, not tomorrow, but it will be you. Until then: rest. Spend. Come back better-armed than you left.

return-reward.speaker: brenna
return-reward.text: One hundred gold from the guild purse — a rank above what the job paid. Two-fifty of experience, for what you earned and what you suffered. I'll enter you on the E-rank board when you next come in.

return-close.speaker: brenna
return-close.text: One last thing. Keep the token on you. It has not finished with you, and you have not finished with it. The sky is grey; so are you. Go.

path-work.speaker: brenna
path-work.text: Work, yes. We had four adventurers take a cave job three weeks ago. None of them came back. The job is still on the board. So — we have work. Not all of it is the kind you'll thank me for.

path-rumor.speaker: brenna
path-rumor.text: Which one? The tower that rings at moonrise. The thing under the Mossbarrow ruins. Or the crack in the sky some nights — if you stand on the east hill after the bells, and you still have the wits to look up.

path-nowhere.speaker: brenna
path-nowhere.text: Then you are among family. Half the people under this roof ran out of roads. Some of us sleep anyway. It passes.

path-silent.speaker: brenna
path-silent.text: Good. You will last longer than most. They walk through that door and start explaining themselves to me before I've finished reading my ledger. As if a name can make them real.

converge.speaker: brenna
converge.text: Whatever brought you, the board still has work, and the wall still has room for names. But before you sign — look at this.

token-reveal.speaker: narrator
token-reveal.text: She sets a small iron token on the counter between you. A line, a curl, a dot — a simple mark scored into the metal. When you look directly at it, it is still. When you look away, you are almost certain the mark has moved.

token-brenna.speaker: brenna
token-brenna.text: Three mornings ago, someone left it in the ink-pot. No one remembers putting it there. It is not ours. And it will not leave — I have tried.

choices.text: Why are you telling me this?
I want nothing to do with that.

path-meaning.speaker: brenna
path-meaning.text: No one still in Ashenvale can read it. There was a scholar — an Elven man called Veyrin — who could. He went into the Greenhollow woods a fortnight ago on a smaller job. He has not come back. I am told the walk is short.

path-why.speaker: brenna
path-why.text: Because you walked in today, which is more than any of my current adventurers have done. Because you have the look of someone still capable of being curious. Do not take it as a compliment. Curious people also die.

path-refuse.speaker: brenna
path-refuse.text: Good answer. The sensible ones live longest. The F-rank board is by the fire — wolves to cull, a dog to find, a merchant needing escort. Honest work. Welcome to the guild.

offer.speaker: brenna
offer.text: Go to Greenhollow. There is a forester — an old man named Orric — who keeps a cabin near the tree-line. Ask him about a scholar. If Veyrin is alive, bring him back. If he is not, bring what he was carrying. And do not tell the others what I showed you today.

final.speaker: brenna
final.text: The F-rank board is by the fire. A wolf cull. An escort. A missing dog. Honest work, if you want it. But whatever you take — keep the token on you. I think it wants to be carried.

close.speaker: narrator
close.text: She returns to her ledger. The token is warm in your hand, though the counter was cold.

brenna-backstory-1.speaker: brenna
brenna-backstory-1.text: I had someone. An adventurer, like you. He went into Mossbarrow seven months ago. I stopped counting the days when I lost the number.

brenna-backstory-1-close.speaker: brenna
brenna-backstory-1-close.text: That is more than I have said to anyone in this hall. Do not make me regret it.

brenna-backstory-2.speaker: brenna
brenna-backstory-2.text: His name was Aldric. He was better than me with a blade and worse than me with words. The Hollow King killed him on the third floor. I found his shield when you cleared it. I have not been able to look at it.

brenna-backstory-2-close.speaker: narrator
brenna-backstory-2-close.text: Brenna turns away. Her hand rests on the edge of the counter — the only part of the gesture that trembles.

brenna-backstory-3.speaker: brenna
brenna-backstory-3.text: I will do what I have always done. Keep the ledger. Light the fire. Wait for someone to walk through that door who is braver than I am. You already did. So I suppose I am waiting for the next one.

brenna-backstory-3-close.speaker: brenna
brenna-backstory-3-close.text: Go. The work does not wait, and neither should you.

brenna-grown.speaker: brenna
brenna-grown.text: Aye. I do. You came in skinny and quiet, and I had you marked for the wall by spring. Now you carry yourself like the room is yours to leave standing. That is not a compliment. It is a measurement.

brenna-grown-2.speaker: brenna
brenna-grown-2.text: I have known three people who walked in like that. One of them is in the ground. One of them is Aldric, also in the ground. The third is still walking. I am hoping for two out of four.

final-gate-offer.speaker: brenna
final-gate-offer.text: You have the Mirror Shard. You have the Flame Amulet. I have been waiting for someone to carry both. It means the seals have broken. The Hollow King, the Drowned Warden — they were fragments. Pieces of something older.

final-gate-offer-2.speaker: brenna
final-gate-offer-2.text: The Crownless One. A king who lost his crown, his throne, his kingdom — and refused to let go. He scattered himself across the world. Every boss you killed freed a piece of his soul. Now he reforms, at The Shattered Coast. The edge of everything.

final-gate-offer-3.speaker: brenna
final-gate-offer-3.text: The path opens east of the Ashfields. Bring both relics. Without them, the way resists you.

final-gate-companion.speaker: brenna
final-gate-companion.text: I have been behind this counter long enough. Aldric would not have stayed. Neither will I.

choices.text: Stay here. Someone needs to keep the ledger.

brenna-joins.speaker: narrator
brenna-joins.text: Brenna reaches beneath the counter. A blade — old, well-kept, wrapped in oiled cloth. She unwraps it with steady hands. The steel remembers her.

brenna-joins-2.speaker: brenna
brenna-joins-2.text: Let us finish what was started. For the guild. For Aldric. For every name on that wall.

brenna-stays.speaker: brenna
brenna-stays.text: You are right. Someone has to write the ending, whichever way it falls. Go. I will be here when you return. I am always here.

final-gate-turnin.speaker: brenna
final-gate-turnin.text: You made it to the coast. Good. Now go deeper. The Throne Beneath waits.

crownless-turnin.speaker: narrator
crownless-turnin.text: Brenna does not speak immediately. She looks at you the way one looks at the last ember of a fire — not with relief, but with the understanding that something has ended that will not begin again.

crownless-turnin-2.speaker: brenna
crownless-turnin-2.text: It is done. The throne is empty. It always was — that was the cruelest part. He fought for something that never existed.

crownless-turnin-3.speaker: brenna
crownless-turnin-3.text: Five hundred gold. One thousand experience. And something the guild has never given before — rest. You have earned it more than anyone I have known.

crownless-turnin-close.speaker: brenna
crownless-turnin-close.text: Rest now. You have earned it more than anyone I have known.
